1397	/-! ### Helper iter-201 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): matrix decomposition and
1398	matrix-collapse on `Ext`.
1399	
1400	For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` between standard free modules over a
1401	commutative ring R, A decomposes as `A = ∑_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} • E_{i,j}` where
1402	`A_{i,j} = (A (Pi.single j 1)) i` is the matrix entry and `E_{i,j}` is the
1403	"elementary" linear map sending `Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1`. From this
1404	decomposition combined with R-bilinearity of `Ext.comp` and the axiom-clean
1405	helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`, we obtain the matrix-collapse
1406	result: if every entry of A lies in `Ann_R N`, then the induced postcomposition
1407	`Ext^p(N, R^m) → Ext^p(N, R^n)` via `mk₀ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.
1408	
1409	This is the key new substrate for closing the **Path B base case `pd M = 1`**
1410	of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula: given a minimal surjection `f : R^n ↠ M`
1411	with `ker f` free of positive rank, the inclusion `ker f ≅ R^k ↪ R^n` is an
1412	R-linear map with entries in 𝔪 (by minimality `ker f ≤ 𝔪 • ⊤`); the
1413	matrix-collapse then forces the LES injectivity needed to conclude
1414	`depth M < depth R`. -/
1415	
1416	/-- The "elementary matrix" linear map `E_{i,j} : R^m →ₗ R^n` sending
1417	`Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1` and all other standard basis vectors to 0. -/
1418	private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
1419	    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
1420	  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) ∘ₗ
1421	    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)
1422	
1423	/-- The elementary map `E_{i,j}` evaluated at `x : R^m` gives `Pi.single i (x j)`. -/
1424	private lemma elemMap_apply {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ)
1425	    (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) (x : Fin m → R) :
1426	    (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
1427	  classical
1428	  change (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R)
1429	    (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) (x j) = Pi.single i (x j)
1430	  rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
1431	  ext k
1432	  rw [Pi.smul_apply]
1433	  by_cases hk : k = i
1434	  · subst hk; rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
1435	  · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, smul_zero]
1436	
1437	/-- Matrix decomposition: every R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` can be written
1438	as a sum `∑_{(i,j)} A (Pi.single j 1) i • elemMap n m i j` of elementary maps
1439	weighted by matrix entries. -/
1440	private lemma linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ℕ}
1441	    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) :
1442	    A = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2 := by
1443	  classical
1444	  refine LinearMap.ext fun (x : Fin m → R) => ?_
1445	  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
1446	  rw [show A x = ∑ j : Fin m, ∑ i : Fin n,
1447	      (A (Pi.single j 1) i) • (Pi.single i (x j) : Fin n → R) from ?_]
1448	  · rw [← Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product_right]
1449	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
1450	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
1451	    rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, elemMap_apply]
1452	  · have hx_decomp : x = ∑ j : Fin m, (x j) • (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m → R) := by
1453	      ext k
1454	      rw [Finset.sum_apply, Finset.sum_eq_single k]
1455	      · simp
1456	      · intro b _ hb
1457	        rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm, smul_zero]
1458	      · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
1459	    conv_lhs => rw [hx_decomp, map_sum]
1460	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
1461	    rw [map_smul]
1462	    ext k
1463	    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
1464	    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
1465	    · rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]
1466	    · intro b _ hb
1467	      rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hb), smul_zero]
1468	    · intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
1469	
1470	/-- **Matrix-collapse on Ext.** For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` whose every
1471	matrix entry `A (Pi.single j 1) i` lies in `Ann_R N`, the postcomposition map
1472	`Ext^p(N, R^m) → Ext^p(N, R^n)` induced by `mk₀ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.
1473	
1474	Proof: write `A = ∑_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} • E_{i,j}` via `linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp`.
1475	Push through `ofHom`, `mk₀`, and `Ext.comp` using `ofHom_sum / mk₀_sum / comp_sum`
1476	plus `ofHom_smul / mk₀_smul / comp_smul`. Each summand becomes
1477	`A_{i,j} • (e.comp (mk₀ (ofHom (elemMap _ _ i j))))`, where the scalar `A_{i,j}`
1478	lies in `Ann_R N`. The existing `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (Stacks
1479	00LP fragment) makes each such scalar action zero. Hence the total sum is zero. -/
1480	private lemma ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator
1481	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
1482	    {N : ModuleCat.{u} R}
1483	    {n m : ℕ}
1484	    (A : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R))
1485	    (hA : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m),
1486	        A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (N : Type u))
1487	    {p : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R)) p) :
1488	    e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
1489	              (ModuleCat.ofHom A :
1490	                ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R)))
1491	          (add_zero p) = 0 := by
1492	  classical
1493	  rw [linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp A]
1494	  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m,
1495	      A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
1496	      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R))
1497	      = ∑ ij : Fin n × Fin m, ModuleCat.ofHom
1498	          (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from by
1499	    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_
1500	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum]
1501	    rfl]
1502	  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_sum]
1503	  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_sum]
1504	  apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
1505	  intro ij _
1506	  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
1507	      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R))
1508	      = A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from by
1509	    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_; rfl]
1510	  rw [show (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
1511	      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) :
1512	        Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m → R)) (ModuleCat.of R (Fin n → R)) 0)
1513	      = A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀
1514	          (ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) from
1515	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) _ _]
1516	  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_smul]
1517	  exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator _ (hA ij.1 ij.2)
1518	
1519	end Module

1521	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1522	
1523	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1524	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1525	```
1526	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1527	```
1528	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1529	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1530	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1531	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1532	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1533	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1534	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1535	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1536	
1537	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
1538	
1539	/-! ### Lane G iter-195 structural carving: inductive-step helper.
1540	
1541	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula is
1542	extracted into the helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` below. This
1543	isolates the entire substrate-gap content of the inductive step into a
1544	single named typed-`sorry` declaration with a precise iter-196+
1545	re-engagement plan, rather than an opaque inline `sorry` at the case-split
1546	site. The main theorem then dispatches the `n > 0` branch by a one-line
1547	`exact` call to the helper.
1548	
1549	Per the iter-195 Lane G directive (OFF-CRITICAL-PATH minimal dispatch),
1550	this is a **structural commit** (option (b): carving with iter-196 re-
1551	engagement timeline). The HARD BAR is met; closure is deferred. -/
1552	
1553	/-- **Lane G iter-195 structural carving (typed-`sorry` substrate-gap helper).**
1554	
1555	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.
1556	
1557	**Blueprint proof shape** (`sec:ab_main` inductive step, Stacks 090V).
1558	Strong induction on `depth(M)`:
1559	
1560	* **Sub-case `depth(M) = 0`.** Let `e := pd_R(M) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1561	  `lemma-add-trivial-complex` (minimal-finite-free-resolution carving),
1562	  `M` admits a minimal resolution
1563	  `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → ⋯ → R^{n_0} → M → 0` with all matrix
1564	  coefficients in `𝔪`. The "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF)
1565	  gives `depth(R) ≥ e`. Iterating `depth_of_short_exact` on the
1566	  short-exact splits `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → K_{e-2} → 0`, …,
1567	  `0 → K_0 → R^{n_0} → M → 0` gives
1568	  `depth(K_{e-c}) ≥ depth(R) - (c - 1)` and finally
1569	  `depth(M) ≥ depth(R) - e`. Since `depth(M) = 0`, also `depth(R) ≤ e`,
1570	  hence `depth(R) = e = (k + 1) + depth(M)`.
1571	
1572	* **Sub-case `depth(M) > 0`.** Pick `x ∈ 𝔪` a common NZD on both `R`
1573	  and `M` (Stacks `lemma-depth-sum-direct-sum` ⟹ `depth(R ⊕ M) > 0`,
1574	  hence a common NZD via prime avoidance over `Ass(R) ∪ Ass(M)`). The
1575	  snake lemma applied to multiplication by `x` on a minimal resolution
1576	  of `M` produces a minimal resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the
1577	  same length, so `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1578	  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`, `depth(R/xR) = depth(R) - 1` and
1579	  `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`, with regular sequences in `𝔪`
1580	  corresponding to those in `𝔪/(x)`, so
1581	  `depth_R(M/xM) = depth_{R/xR}(M/xM)`. The IH on `M/xM` over `R/xR`
1582	  gives `(k + 1) + (depth(M) - 1) = depth(R) - 1`, i.e.
1583	  `(k + 1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`.
1584

1782	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
1783	
1784	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
1785	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
1786	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
1787	
1788	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
1789	
1790	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
1791	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
1792	
1793	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
1794	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
1795	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
1796	this is the upstream gap-fill.
1797	
1798	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
1799	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
1800	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
1801	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
1802	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1803	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
1804	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
1805	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
1806	  depth_eq_krullDim :
1807	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
1808	
1809	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
1810	
1811	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
1812	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
1813	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
1814	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
1815	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
1816	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
1817	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
1818	
1819	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
1820	
1821	namespace CohenMacaulay
1822	
1823	/-! ### Helper 1 (axiom-clean): length-bound on regular sequences
1824	
1825	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, every `R`-regular sequence has length at most
1826	`ringKrullDim R`. This is the **upper bound** half of Stacks 00OD: it is the
1827	specialisation of the equality
1828	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) + rs.length = ringKrullDim R`
1829	(`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) to the observation that
1830	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) ≥ 0` whenever the quotient is nontrivial, which it
1831	is precisely because `IsRegular` rules out `rs • ⊤ = ⊤`.
1832	
1833	iter-181 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. -/
1834	private lemma length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular
1835	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1836	    {rs : List R} (h : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs) :
1837	    (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim R := by
1838	  have heq := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs h
1839	  have hntq : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) := by
1840	    rw [Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
1841	    intro habs
1842	    apply h.top_ne_smul
1843	    change (⊤ : Submodule R R) = (Ideal.ofList rs) • ⊤
1844	    rw [habs]; simp
1845	  have hnn : (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) :=
1846	    ringKrullDim_nonneg_of_nontrivial
1847	  calc (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞)
1848	      = 0 + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by simp
1849	    _ ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by gcongr
1850	    _ = ringKrullDim R := heq
1851	
1852	/-! ### Helper 1.5 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent-image of `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`
1853	
1854	For a local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`, the image of `x` in the
1855	cotangent space `𝔪.Cotangent` is nonzero. This is the key positivity input
1856	for the iter-187 cotangent dim-drop lemma below.
1857	
1858	Axiom-clean: direct from `Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero`. -/
1859	private lemma toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq
1860	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1861	    (x : R) (hx : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1862	    (hxnotsq : x ∉ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ^ 2) :
1863	    (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).toCotangent
1864	        (⟨x, hx⟩ : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Ideal R)) ≠ 0 := by
1865	  intro habs
1866	  exact hxnotsq
1867	    ((Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero (I := IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ⟨x, hx⟩).mp habs)
1868	
1869	/-! ### Helper 2.0 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent dim-drop on `R ⧸ (x)`
1870	
1871	**Statement.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the
1872	cotangent space of `R / (x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`:
1873	```
1874	finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
1875	```
1876	where `κ = R / 𝔪` and `κ' = (R/(x)) / 𝔪'` are the two residue fields
1877	(canonically isomorphic via the natural quotient `R/𝔪 ≃ (R/(x))/𝔪'`).
1878	
1879	**Role.** This is the cotangent-space dim-drop building block for
1880	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` below: once Stacks 00NQ
1881	(regular local ⟹ domain) is filled in, this dim-drop is what upgrades
1882	`R/(x)` of dimension `k` back to `IsRegularLocalRing` via
1883	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
1884	
1885	**Proof structure** (Bourbaki-style via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`):
1886	1. The R-algebra map `π : R → R/(x)` induces a κ-linear surjection of
1887	   cotangent spaces `f : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent` via
1888	   `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq` (which applies because
1889	   `(maximalIdeal R).comap π = ker π ⊔ maximalIdeal R = (x) ⊔ 𝔪 = 𝔪`).
1890	2. The kernel of `f` is the R-submodule generated by the image `x̄` of `x`
1891	   in `𝔪.Cotangent` (via `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` — the
1892	   kernel is `(Submodule.comap 𝔪.subtype ((x) ⊓ 𝔪)).map 𝔪.toCotangent`
1893	   which collapses to `Submodule.span R {x̄}` because `(x) ⊆ 𝔪`).
1894	3. The R-action on 𝔪.Cotangent factors through κ (it's a κ-vector space),
1895	   so this is also `Submodule.span κ {x̄}`.
1896	4. `x ∉ 𝔪²` ⟺ `x̄ ≠ 0` (Helper 1.5), giving
1897	   `finrank κ (Submodule.span κ {x̄}) = 1` via `finrank_span_singleton`.
1898	5. Combining via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` and the
1899	   `LinearMap.quotKerEquivOfSurjective` isomorphism, we get the formula.
1900	   The residue-field switch `κ → κ'` is handled by
1901	   `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`, which
1902	   reduces the κ-finrank to spanFinrank (a ring-theoretic invariant) and
1903	   the spanFinrank-equality is purely numeric.
1904	
1905	**iter-187 deliverable**: structural scaffold with the assembled body left
1906	as a single named typed sorry (the κ-subspace identification step). The
1907	preparatory lemma `toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq` lands axiom-clean.
1908	
1909	**iter-188+** body fill-in: instantiate steps (1)-(5) above. Key Mathlib API:
1910	* `Ideal.mapCotangent`, `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq`,
1911	  `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
1912	* `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
1913	* `finrank_span_singleton` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
1914	* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`
1915	  (Mathlib b80f227 ✓) — bridges κ-finrank and κ'-finrank via spanFinrank.
1916	
1917	The substantive ring-theoretic content is concentrated in the kernel
1918	description (step 2) and the κ vs κ' identification (step 5); both
1919	have axiom-clean Mathlib infrastructure but require a careful
1920	multi-step instantiation. -/
1921	private theorem finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ
1922	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]

1273	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]

1301	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1302	
1303	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1304	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1305	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1306	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1307	
1308	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1309	
1310	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1311	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1312	
1313	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1314	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1315	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1316	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1317	
1318	The proof combines two steps:
1319	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1320	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1321	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1322	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1323	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1324	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1325	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1326	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1327	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1328	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1329	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1330	
1331	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1332	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1333	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1334	
1335	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1336	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1337	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1338	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1339	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1340	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1341	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1342	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]

1345	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1346	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]

1348	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by

1350	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine

1352	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1353	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1354	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _

1372	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).

1374	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF

1378	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1379	
1380	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1381	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1382	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1383	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1384	
1385	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1386	
1387	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1388	codim 1.**
1389	
1390	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1391	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1392	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1393	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1394	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1395	
1396	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1397	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1398	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1399	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1400	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1401	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1402	
1403	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1404	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1405	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1406	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1407	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1408	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1409	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1410	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1411	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1412	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1413	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1414	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1415	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1416	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1417	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1418	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1419	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1420	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1421	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1422	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1423	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1424	  --
1425	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1426	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1427	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1428	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1429	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1430	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1431	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1432	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1433	  --   the domain of definition.
1434	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1435	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1436	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1437	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1438	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1439	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1440	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1441	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1442	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1443	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1444	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1445	  --   not packaged.
1446	  --
1447	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1448	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1449	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne
1450	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form).
1451	  sorry

1453	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1454	
1455	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1456	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1457	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1458	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1459	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1460	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1461	witnesses are constructed in this project).
1462	
1463	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
1464	
1465	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
1466	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
1467	
1468	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
1469	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
1470	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
1471	
1472	which requires the pullback machinery
1473	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
1474	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
1475	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
1476	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
1477	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
1478	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
1479	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
1480	
1481	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
1482	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
1483	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
1484	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
1485	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
1486	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
1487	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
1488	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
1489	the pullback machinery lands.
1490	
1491	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
1492	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
1493	
1494	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
1495	
1496	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
1497	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
1498	are equivalent:
1499	
1500	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
1501	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
1502	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
1503	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
1504	
1505	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
1506	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
1507	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
1508	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
1509	construct partial-map representatives).
1510	
1511	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
1512	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
1513	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
1514	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
1515	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
1516	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
1517	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
1518	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1519	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1520	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1521	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1522	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1523	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1524	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1525	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1526	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
1527	    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
1528	      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
1529	        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
1530	  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
1531	  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩
1532	
1533	end RationalMap
1534

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
64	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
65	  [IsProper C.hom]
66	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
67	
68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
78	
79	namespace IsAlbanese
80	
81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h
85	
86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1

92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}

100	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
101	universal morphisms. -/
102	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
103	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
104	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
105	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
106	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
107	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
108	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
109	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
110	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
111	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
112	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
113	    simp
114	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
115	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
116	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
117	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
118	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
119	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
120	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
121	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
122	    simp
123	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
124	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
125	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
126	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
127	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
128	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
129	
130	end IsAlbanese

142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve

161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom

170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom

172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible

174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred

201	    JacobianWitness C where
202	  J := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))

208	  geomIrred := geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec k

212	    -- For every `k`-rational marked point `P`, exhibit the Albanese datum for
213	    -- the pointed curve `(C, P)` with `J = 𝟙_` (blueprint C.2 / C.3).
214	    intro P
215	    -- The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map `toUnit C`.
216	    refine ⟨toUnit C, ?_, ?_⟩
217	    · -- Pointed condition: both `P ≫ toUnit C` and `η[𝟙_]` are maps `𝟙_ ⟶ 𝟙_`,

219	      exact toUnit_unique _ _
220	    · -- Universal factorisation: given `f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]`, the

222	      intro A _ _ _ _ f _hf

2024	        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
2025	      apply le_antisymm
2026	      · rw [Submodule.span_le]
2027	        rintro y hy
2028	        rcases hy with rfl | hy
2029	        · exact hx
2030	        · have hy_V : y ∈ V := Finset.mem_of_mem_erase (by exact_mod_cast hy)

2035	        by_cases hv_eq : v = v₀

2038	          right
2039	          exact_mod_cast Finset.mem_erase.mpr ⟨hv_eq, by exact_mod_cast hv⟩

2041	    -- First, 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx 𝔪 R, and mkx x = 0.
2042	    set mkx : R →+* (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) := Ideal.Quotient.mk _ with hmkx_def

2044	      Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr (Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x)

2047	        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by

2053	        Ideal.map mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) := by
2054	      conv_rhs => rw [← hcomap_eq]
2055	      exact (Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective _).symm

2074	          · exact Submodule.zero_mem _

2076	        · exact Ideal.span_mono (Set.subset_insert _ _)

2092	  · -- (≥): (𝔪 R).spanFinrank ≤ (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1.
2093	    -- Lift-and-cons strategy: a min gen set T of 𝔪 (R/(x)) lifts to T_lift ⊆
2094	    -- 𝔪 R via `Function.surjInv` of `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the union
2095	    -- `T_lift ∪ {x}` generates 𝔪 R since 𝔪 R = (Ideal.span {x}) ⊔ (lift of 𝔪').
2096	    classical
2097	    set R' : Type u := R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} with hR'_def
2098	    let mkx : R →+* R' := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
2099	    let g : R' → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2100	    have hg : ∀ y, mkx (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
2101	    -- Get min gen finset of 𝔪'.
2102	    have h𝔪'_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _

2104	      Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank h𝔪'_fg
2105	    -- T : Finset R', T.card = spanFinrank 𝔪', span R' T = 𝔪'.
2106	    let T_lift : Finset R := T.image g
2107	    let U : Finset R := insert x T_lift
2108	    -- Step A: U generates 𝔪 R via the comap identification.
2109	    -- Comap mkx (𝔪 R') = 𝔪 R, since R is local and mkx is surjective.
2110	    have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') =
2111	        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
2112	      have hmax : (Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R')).IsMaximal :=
2113	        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2114	      exact (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax
2115	    have hker_eq : RingHom.ker mkx = Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.mk_ker
2116	    -- Step B: 𝔪 R = (Ideal.span T_lift) ⊔ (Ideal.span {x}).
2117	    -- Compute: comap mkx (map mkx (Ideal.span T_lift)) = Ideal.span T_lift ⊔ ker mkx.
2118	    have hmap_T_lift : Ideal.map mkx (Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R)) =
2119	        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R' := by
2120	      rw [Ideal.map_span]
2121	      -- mkx '' T_lift = T (as sets).
2122	      have him : mkx '' (T_lift : Set R) = (T : Set R') := by
2123	        ext y
2124	        simp only [Set.mem_image, Finset.coe_image, T_lift]
2125	        refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
2126	        · rintro ⟨a, ⟨b, hbT, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
2127	          rw [hg b]; exact hbT
2128	        · intro hyT
2129	          exact ⟨g y, ⟨y, hyT, rfl⟩, hg y⟩
2130	      rw [him]; exact hT_span
2131	    have h𝔪R_decomp : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R =
2132	        Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by
2133	      calc IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R
2134	          = Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') := hcomap_eq.symm
2135	        _ = Ideal.comap mkx (Ideal.map mkx (Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R))) := by
2136	              rw [hmap_T_lift]
2137	        _ = Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ RingHom.ker mkx := by
2138	              rw [Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective' mkx Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective]
2139	        _ = Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by rw [hker_eq]
2140	    -- Step C: span R U = Ideal.span T_lift ⊔ Ideal.span {x}.

2142	        Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by
2143	      have hUeq : (↑U : Set R) = (↑T_lift : Set R) ∪ {x} := by
2144	        change ((insert x T_lift : Finset R) : Set R) = _

2146	      rw [hUeq, Submodule.span_union]

2149	        = Submodule.spanFinrank (Submodule.span R (↑U : Set R)) := by

2151	      _ ≤ (↑U : Set R).ncard :=
2152	          Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite U.finite_toSet

2154	      _ ≤ T_lift.card + 1 := by
2155	          have := Finset.card_insert_le x T_lift
2156	          simpa [U] using this
2157	      _ ≤ T.card + 1 := by
2158	          have hle : T_lift.card ≤ T.card := Finset.card_image_le
2159	          omega
2160	      _ = Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') + 1 := by

2163	/-! ### Helper 2 (substantive typed `sorry`): system-of-parameters as a regular sequence
2164	
2165	For a regular local ring `R` of Krull dimension `d = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`,
2166	a minimal generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of the maximal ideal `𝔪` is an `R`-regular
2167	sequence. This is the **lower bound** half of Stacks 00OD.
2168	
2169	The proof in Stacks 00OD uses:
2170	1. A regular local ring is an integral domain (Stacks 00NQ).
2171	2. For each `c < d`, the quotient `R / (x_1, …, x_c)` is again a regular local
2172	   ring, of Krull dimension `d - c` (Krull's principal ideal theorem +
2173	   `lemma-one-equation`).
2174	
2175	Step (1) — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R` — is **not present in Mathlib at
2176	the pinned commit** (`b80f227`); a `lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is
2177	a domain" returns nothing relevant. Step (2) requires the regular-quotient
2178	inductive structure (Stacks 00NQ + height-one quotient regularity), which is
2179	similarly absent. Both are substantive multi-iter content.
2180	
2181	The signature here is the non-tautological existence statement: a list `rs : List R`
2182	with `rs.length = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`, `rs ⊆ maximalIdeal R`, and
2183	`IsRegular R rs`. Downstream, `of_regular` consumes this directly to close the
2184	`depth ≥ d` lower bound.
2185	
2186	iter-185 Lane G **STRUCTURAL SCAFFOLD**: the main lemma is structurally
2187	complete by strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`. The inductive step is
2188	factored into `regularLocal_inductive_step` (axiom-clean: lift-and-cons
2189	assembly via Mathlib's `IsRegular.cons'`) plus the Mathlib-gap helper
2190	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (typed `sorry`,
2191	Stacks 00NQ + 00NU consolidated). The substrate work (`IsRegularLocalRing ⟹
2192	IsDomain` + Krull's PIT for quotient-by-minimal-generator) is genuinely
2193	multi-iter; the iter-185 progress is the structural extraction of the
2194	Mathlib gap into a narrow, well-typed helper, leaving the assembly
2195	axiom-clean for the gap-fill iteration to finish in `~5 LOC` once the
2196	helper closes. -/
2197	
2198	/-! ### Narrow substrate gap (Stacks 00NQ): regular local ring is a domain
2199	
2200	The Auslander–Buchsbaum-route closure of "regular local ring is Cohen–Macaulay"
2201	ultimately reduces to **Stacks 00NQ**: every regular local Noetherian ring
2202	is an integral domain. Mathlib at the pinned commit `b80f227` does not expose
2203	this implication (`lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is a domain"
2204	returns nothing relevant; `lean_loogle` for `IsRegularLocalRing _ → IsDomain _`
2205	returns empty).
2206	
2207	Stacks 00NQ proof sketch (induction on `dim R = spanFinrank 𝔪`):
2208	* Base `dim R = 0`: then `𝔪 = ⊥`, hence `R` is a field, hence a domain.
2209	* Step `dim R = d + 1 ≥ 1`:
2210	  - Pick `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` (axiom-clean via Nakayama; see
2211	    `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos` below).
2212	  - By the cotangent dim-drop (`finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ`,
2213	    iter-188 Lane G1 closure) **plus** Krull's principal ideal theorem
2214	    (`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_jacobson`
2215	    gives the `≥` half), `R / (x)` is regular local of dim `d`.
2216	  - By induction, `R / (x)` is a domain, hence `(x)` is a prime ideal of `R`.
2217	  - Let `𝔭` be a minimal prime of `R` with `𝔭 ⊆ (x)`. For `y ∈ 𝔭`, write
2218	    `y = xz`; since `𝔭` is prime and `x ∉ 𝔭` (else `x ∈ 𝔭 ⊆ (x)` doesn't
2219	    immediately give a contradiction but combined with `dim R/(x) = d` and
2220	    `x ∈ 𝔭` height-zero we get `𝔭 = 0` which is the conclusion), we get
2221	    `z ∈ 𝔭`. Hence `𝔭 ⊆ x 𝔭`, so iterating, `𝔭 ⊆ ∩ x^n 𝔭 ⊆ ∩ 𝔪^n = 0`
2222	    (Krull intersection). So `𝔭 = 0`, and `R` is a domain.
2223	
2224	Full formalization is ~300 LOC (involves Krull's PIT, prime avoidance,
2225	Krull intersection, minimal-primes-are-finite); deferred to a future iter
2226	or Mathlib upstream contribution. -/
2227	
2228	/-- **Axiom-clean Nakayama witness.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
2229	with `spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1`, there exists `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
2230	
2231	This is the "cotangent space is nonzero" content: by Nakayama, if `𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪²`
2232	then `𝔪 = 0` (so `spanFinrank 𝔪 = 0`), contradicting the hypothesis. -/
2233	private lemma exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos
2234	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2235	    (h : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank) :
2236	    ∃ x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R, x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := by
2237	  -- By contradiction: assume 𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪², then by Nakayama 𝔪 = 0, but spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1.
2238	  by_contra h_neg
2239	  push Not at h_neg
2240	  have h𝔪_le_sq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := h_neg
2241	  -- 𝔪² = 𝔪 • 𝔪.
2242	  have hsq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ^ 2
2243	      = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
2244	    rw [sq, ← Ideal.smul_eq_mul]
2245	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R).FG :=
2246	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
2247	  -- Use Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot with N = ⊥.
2248	  have hjac : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ≤ (⊥ : Ideal R).jacobson :=
2249	    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
2250	  have h_le_smul : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R)
2251	      ≤ ⊥ ⊔ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) •
2252	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
2253	    rw [bot_sup_eq, ← hsq]; exact h𝔪_le_sq
2254	  have h𝔪_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ≤ ⊥ :=
2255	    Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot h𝔪_fg hjac h_le_smul
2256	  have h𝔪_eq_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) = ⊥ := le_bot_iff.mp h𝔪_bot
2257	  have h_span : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0 := by
2258	    rw [h𝔪_eq_bot]; exact Submodule.spanFinrank_bot
2259	  omega

2261	/-! ### Helper for Stacks 00NQ base case (iter-190 Lane G, axiom-clean).
2262	
2263	For a Noetherian local ring `R` with `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0`, the
2264	ring `R` is a field, hence a domain. The maximal ideal collapses to `⊥` via
2265	`Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot` (under FG), and
2266	`IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` upgrades the resulting field-by-
2267	trivial-maximal-ideal characterisation to `IsField R`, from which
2268	`IsField.isDomain` gives `IsDomain R`. -/
2269	private lemma isDomain_of_isLocalRing_of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_zero
2270	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2271	    (h : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0) : IsDomain R := by
2272	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).FG :=
2273	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
2274	  have h𝔪_bot : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R = ⊥ :=
2275	    (Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot h𝔪_fg).mp h
2276	  have hField : IsField R :=
2277	    IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr h𝔪_bot
2278	  exact hField.isDomain

2281	
2282	For a regular local Noetherian ring `R` of `spanFinrank 𝔪 = k + 1` and
2283	`x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the quotient `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` is again a regular local

2296	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)

2301	    ∃ _ : IsRegularLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
2302	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by
2303	  -- Step 1: instances on R/(x).

2305	    (IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mpr hu) hxMem

2326	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by

2346	    exact_mod_cast h.symm
2347	  -- Step 4: extract `ringKrullDim R/(x) = k` from hKrullDimLE + upper bound.
2348	  -- Upper bound: `ringKrullDim R/(x) ≤ spanFinrank 𝔪' = k`.
2349	  have h_dim_upper : ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≤
2350	      ((k : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2351	    refine le_trans (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal _) ?_
2352	    rw [hspan_R'_eq_k]
2353	  -- Adding 1 to both sides of h_dim_upper:
2354	  -- `ringKrullDim R/(x) + 1 ≤ (k:WithBot) + 1 = (k+1:WithBot) = ringKrullDim R`.
2355	  have h_add_le : ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1 ≤
2356	      ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2357	    calc ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1
2358	        ≤ ((k : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) + 1 := by gcongr
2359	      _ = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by push_cast; ring
2360	  -- Combined with hKrullDimLE via hR_dim → equation in WithBot.
2361	  have h_add_eq : ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1
2362	      = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2363	    rw [hR_dim] at hKrullDimLE
2364	    exact le_antisymm h_add_le hKrullDimLE
2365	  -- Extract `ringKrullDim R/(x) = k : WithBot ℕ∞` via `WithBot.add_eq_coe`.
2366	  have hR'_dim_eq : ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))
2367	      = ((k : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2368	    obtain ⟨a', b', ha', hb', hab⟩ := WithBot.add_eq_coe.mp h_add_eq
2369	    rw [← ha']
2370	    have hb_eq : b' = (1 : ℕ∞) := by
2371	      have h1 : ((b' : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) = ((1 : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2372	        rw [hb']; simp
2373	      exact_mod_cast h1
2374	    have ha_eq : a' = (k : ℕ∞) := by
2375	      rw [hb_eq] at hab
2376	      have hcast2 : a' + 1 = (k : ℕ∞) + 1 := by exact_mod_cast hab
2377	      have hne_top : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤ := by simp
2378	      exact WithTop.add_right_cancel hne_top hcast2
2379	    exact_mod_cast ha_eq
2380	  -- Step 5: spanFinrank 𝔪' = k = ringKrullDim R/(x) → IsRegularLocalRing R/(x).
2381	  have hRLR : IsRegularLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) := by
2382	    apply IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
2383	    rw [hspan_R'_eq_k, hR'_dim_eq]
2384	  exact ⟨hNT, hLR, hRLR, hspan_R'_eq_k⟩

2386	/-! ### Helper iter-191 Lane G (axiom-clean): zero-divisor witness from a minimal prime
2387	
2388	For a commutative ring `R` and a minimal prime `𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R`, every
2389	element of `𝔭` is a zero-divisor in `R`. Concretely: for any `x ∈ 𝔭`, there
2390	exists `y ∈ R, y ≠ 0` with `x * y = 0`.
2391	
2392	Proof: minimal primes are disjoint from non-zero-divisors via
2393	`Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes`, so `x ∈ 𝔭` ⟹
2394	`x ∉ nonZeroDivisors R` ⟹ `∃ y ≠ 0, x * y = 0`.
2395	
2396	This packages the Stacks-00NQ-relevant first step for the `x ∈ 𝔭` case of
2397	`isDomain_of_regularLocal`: when `(x)` is a minimal prime, `x` is a zero-divisor,
2398	so the obstruction reduces to derivation of a contradiction from the
2399	zero-divisor witness (using the regular-local structure of `R` and `R/(x)`). -/
2400	private lemma exists_ne_zero_mul_eq_zero_of_mem_minimalPrimes
2401	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {𝔭 : Ideal R} (h𝔭 : 𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R)
2402	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ 𝔭) :
2403	    ∃ y : R, y ≠ 0 ∧ x * y = 0 := by
2404	  have hdisj : Disjoint (𝔭 : Set R) (nonZeroDivisors R : Set R) :=
2405	    Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes h𝔭
2406	  have hxNot : x ∉ nonZeroDivisors R := fun hxNZD =>
2407	    (Set.disjoint_left.mp hdisj) hx hxNZD
2408	  have hExistsZD : ¬ ∀ z, x * z = 0 → z = 0 := fun h => hxNot <| by
2409	    rw [mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff]
2410	    refine ⟨h, fun z hz => h z (by rw [mul_comm]; exact hz)⟩
2411	  push Not at hExistsZD
2412	  obtain ⟨y, hxy, hy⟩ := hExistsZD
2413	  exact ⟨y, hy, hxy⟩
2414	
2415	/-! ### Helper iter-191 Lane G (substantive typed sorry): `(x)` is not a minimal prime
2416	    in the regular-local inductive step
2417	
2418	For a regular local Noetherian ring `R` of `spanFinrank 𝔪 = k + 1 ≥ 1` and
2419	`x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the ideal `Ideal.span {x}` is *not* a minimal prime of `R`.
2420	This is the substantive remaining content of Stacks 00NQ after the iter-190
2421	case-split scaffold isolated the obstruction to this single case.
2422	
2423	**Statement framing.** The lemma takes the strong-induction hypothesis
2424	`hIH` as an explicit argument (universally quantified over the ring `R'` at
2425	dimension `k`), so it can be invoked inside `isDomain_of_regularLocal`'s
2426	`succ` arm without requiring `IsDomain R` (which is the goal we are
2427	proving). Concretely `hIH` provides `IsDomain (R / (x))` since the quotient
2428	is regular local of `spanFinrank = k` (via
2429	`regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq`).
2430	
2431	**Proof routes (iter-192+ targets).** Three project-side routes have been
2432	considered, all blocked on substrate work beyond Mathlib `b80f227`:
2433	* (i) **Graded-ring approach** (the Stacks 00NQ proof). Build the associated
2434	  graded ring `gr_𝔪 R := ⨁_n 𝔪^n / 𝔪^{n+1}`; prove the surjection
2435	  `κ[X_1,…,X_{k+1}] ↠ gr_𝔪 R` (from a regular system of parameters) is an
2436	  isomorphism (via Hilbert-Samuel function or cotangent count); deduce
2437	  `gr_𝔪 R` is a domain; conclude `R` is a domain via leading-term
2438	  multiplicativity + Krull intersection. ~500–800 LOC.
2439	* (ii) **Cohen-completion bridge.** Pass to `R̂` at `𝔪`, which is regular
2440	  local of same dimension. By the Cohen structure theorem,
2441	  `R̂ ≅ κ̂[[T_1,…,T_{k+1}]]` is a power-series ring over a complete field,
2442	  hence a domain. By flatness of `R → R̂`, the injection `R ↪ R̂` shows `R`
2443	  is a domain. ~400–600 LOC (Cohen structure thm is the big substrate
2444	  cost).
2445	* (iii) **Direct prime-avoidance + Krull-intersection contradiction.** From
2446	  the zero-divisor witness `y ≠ 0, x * y = 0` (via
2447	  `exists_ne_zero_mul_eq_zero_of_mem_minimalPrimes`) and Krull intersection
2448	  `⋂_n (x)^n = ⊥`, write `y = x^m * z` with `z ∉ (x)` and `m < ∞`. Then
2449	  `x^{m+1} * z = 0` and, since `R/(x)` is a domain by IH, `z` represents a
2450	  non-zero-divisor in `R/(x)`. The remaining substantive step is a
2451	  "pull-back" / lifting argument showing `x` must be regular on `R`,
2452	  contradicting the zero-divisor witness. Estimated ~200–300 LOC modulo
2453	  Mathlib's regular-sequence-via-localization-and-completion infrastructure.
2454	
2455	iter-191 Lane G lands the *structural extraction* of this lemma: the typed
2456	sorry is now narrow and well-typed, with all dependent instances in scope,
2457	ready for a future iter's substrate work. The HARD BAR iter-191 outcome —
2458	`exists_ne_zero_mul_eq_zero_of_mem_minimalPrimes` axiom-clean — is the
2459	*first* helper unlocking route (iii). -/
2460	private lemma notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ
2461	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2462	    [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
2463	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
2464	    (x : R) (hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
2465	    (hxNotSq : x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2)
2466	    (hIH : ∀ (R' : Type u) [CommRing R'] [IsLocalRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R']
2467	            [IsRegularLocalRing R'],
2468	            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').spanFinrank = k → IsDomain R') :
2469	    Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) ∉ minimalPrimes R := by
2470	  intro hmin
2471	  -- Step 1: x is a zero-divisor in R via the axiom-clean Helper A.
2472	  have hxIn : x ∈ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) :=
2473	    Ideal.subset_span (Set.mem_singleton x)
2474	  obtain ⟨y, hy_ne, hxy⟩ :=
2475	    exists_ne_zero_mul_eq_zero_of_mem_minimalPrimes hmin hxIn
2476	  -- Step 2: bring `R/(x)` into scope as a regular local ring of `spanFinrank = k`,
2477	  -- and apply IH to obtain `IsDomain (R/(x))`. This packages the substrate that
2478	  -- routes (i)/(ii)/(iii) all need to close the residual.
2479	  obtain ⟨hNT, hLR, hRLR, hdim_quot⟩ :=
2480	    regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq hdim x hxMem hxNotSq
2481	  haveI : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) := hNT
2482	  haveI : IsLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) := hLR
2483	  haveI : IsRegularLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) := hRLR
2484	  haveI hDomain_quot : IsDomain (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2485	    hIH (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) hdim_quot
2486	  -- iter-192 Lane G closure (prime-avoidance route).
2487	  --
2488	  -- Strategy: use the IH-as-universal-quantifier-over-rings hypothesis to
2489	  -- prove `IsDomain R` directly, then derive contradiction from `(x) ∈
2490	  -- minimalPrimes R` + `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
2491	  --
2492	  -- Concretely: pick a *fresh* witness `x' ∈ 𝔪 \ (𝔪² ∪ ⋃ minimalPrimes R)`
2493	  -- via prime avoidance (`Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`).  Then:
2494	  --   * `R/(x')` is regular local of `spanFinrank = k` via the iter-190
2495	  --     helper `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq`,
2496	  --   * `IsDomain (R/(x'))` via `hIH`,
2497	  --   * `(x')` is prime,
2498	  --   * a minimal prime `𝔭' ⊆ (x')` exists (`Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le`),
2499	  --     and `x' ∉ 𝔭'` (since `x'` avoids all minimal primes), so for `y ∈ 𝔭'
2500	  --     ⊆ (x')` we get `y = x' · z` with `z ∈ 𝔭'`, hence `𝔭' ⊆ x' · 𝔭' ⊆
2501	  --     jacobson R · 𝔭'`,
2502	  --   * Nakayama yields `𝔭' = ⊥`, so `⊥ ∈ minimalPrimes R` is prime, hence
2503	  --     `IsDomain R`.
2504	  -- In a domain, `(⊥ : Ideal R).minimalPrimes = {⊥}`, so `hmin` forces
2505	  -- `Ideal.span {x} = ⊥`, hence `x = 0 ∈ 𝔪²`, contradicting `hxNotSq`.
2506	  classical
2507	  set 𝔪 : Ideal R := IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R with h𝔪_def
2508	  -- spanFinrank-positivity follows from hdim.
2509	  have hpos : 0 < 𝔪.spanFinrank := by rw [h𝔪_def, hdim]; omega
2510	  -- Step P1: enumerate the avoidance set `S = {𝔪²} ∪ minimalPrimes R`.
2511	  have hMP_fin : (minimalPrimes R).Finite := minimalPrimes.finite_of_isNoetherianRing R
2512	  let S : Set (Ideal R) := insert (𝔪 ^ 2) (minimalPrimes R)
2513	  have hS_fin : S.Finite := hMP_fin.insert _
2514	  -- Step P2: each element of S other than `𝔪²` is prime.
2515	  have hp : ∀ i ∈ S, i ≠ (𝔪 ^ 2) → i ≠ (𝔪 ^ 2) → i.IsPrime := by
2516	    intro i hi h₁ _
2517	    simp only [S, Set.mem_insert_iff] at hi
2518	    rcases hi with hi | hi
2519	    · exact absurd hi h₁
2520	    · exact Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime hi
2521	  -- Step P3: `𝔪` is not contained in any element of `S`.
2522	  have h_nle : ∀ i ∈ S, ¬ ((𝔪 : Set R) ⊆ (i : Set R)) := by
2523	    intro i hi habs
2524	    simp only [S, Set.mem_insert_iff] at hi
2525	    rcases hi with rfl | hi
2526	    · -- 𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪² contradicts hpos via `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos`.
2527	      obtain ⟨x₀, hx₀Mem, hx₀NotSq⟩ := exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos hpos
2528	      exact hx₀NotSq (habs hx₀Mem)
2529	    · -- i ∈ minimalPrimes R, 𝔪 ⊆ i ⟹ i = 𝔪 (since i ⊆ 𝔪 always), then 𝔪

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/

9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31	
32	## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
35	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
36	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	
39	The 6 pinned declarations are:
40	
41	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
42	   regular morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
43	   `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`. Encoded against the file-internal
44	   placeholder `Pic0.jacobianScheme` for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (the A.3 upstream
45	   has not yet been split out; see §0 below).
46	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) —
47	   the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C` of the curve `C`, as a `k̄`-scheme.

16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map

23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.

13	is the iter-183 Lane M deliverable per
14	`analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md` Decision 2, with blueprint chapter

17	The four declarations are:

19	1. `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding` — for `X` a topological space and
20	   `U ⊆ X` open, `coheight z (in X) = coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in U)` (both wrt the
21	   specialisation preorder, pinned explicitly via `@`).
22	2. `Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum` — for `R` a `CommRingCat` and
23	   `p : Spec R`, `coheight p (in Spec R) = height ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩
24	   (in PrimeSpectrum R)`.
25	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` — the bridge:

27	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one` — codim-1
28	   wrapper: `coheight z = 1 → Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z)`.
29	
30	The first three lemmas are natural Mathlib upstream candidates; the fourth is
31	the project-facing consumer wrapper used in
32	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (`hreg_dim` Krull-dim conjunct) and

36	open Order TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory

42	/-- **Open subsets preserve `Order.coheight`** for points of a topological
43	space carrying the specialisation preorder. Both `Order.coheight` instances
44	are pinned to `specializationPreorder` explicitly (via `@`) so the statement
45	is well-defined even when `X` carries no other `Preorder` instance.
46	
47	Forward bound: `Subtype.val` is continuous, hence spec-monotone, and is
48	injective; therefore strictly monotone. Reverse bound: every generisation of a
49	point in an open set lies in that open (`Specializes.mem_open`), so a chain
50	starting at `z` in `X` lifts to a chain in `↥U` of the same length. -/
51	lemma coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding
52	    {X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] {U : Set X} (hU : IsOpen U)
53	    (z : X) (hz : z ∈ U) :
54	    @Order.coheight X (specializationPreorder X) z
55	      = @Order.coheight U (specializationPreorder U) ⟨z, hz⟩ := by
56	  letI : Preorder X := specializationPreorder X
57	  letI : Preorder ↥U := specializationPreorder ↥U
58	  -- `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is monotone for the spec preorder (since it is
59	  -- continuous), and is injective; hence strictly monotone.
60	  have hmono : Monotone (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) :=

25	The chapter strategy (Hartshorne III §2): an injective resolution of a
26	flasque sheaf yields a flasque quotient at each stage; the
27	global-sections functor is exact on a flasque-to-flasque-to-flasque
28	short exact sequence; hence the right-derived global-sections functor

30	abstract flasque-vanishing to the closed-point skyscraper sheaf, which
31	is flasque because it is the pushforward, along the closed embedding of

2665	    exact haux _ R rfl

2681	      ih (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) hdim_quot
2682	    -- Step 4: (x) is prime in R (R/(x) is a domain).

2687	      (I := (⊥ : Ideal R)) (J := Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) bot_le

2707	          Ring.jacobson R • (𝔭 : Submodule R R) := by

2800	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1) :

2802	      ∃ _ : IsRegularLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}),

2806	    exists_isSMulRegular_notMemSq_of_regularLocal_succ (k := k) hdim
2807	  refine ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, ?_⟩
2808	  -- Step 2: assemble the structural instances on `R/(x)`.

2810	    (IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mpr hu) hxMem
2811	  have hspan_ne_top : (Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≠ ⊤ :=

2813	  haveI : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2814	    Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hspan_ne_top

2823	    rw [← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R]
2824	    exact hdim

2826	      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)))
2827	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))) = k := by

2870	/-- **Lane G inductive step (iter-185 structural scaffold).** Packages the
2871	inductive step of Stacks 00OD: given a regular local ring `R` of dimension
2872	`k + 1`, plus the inductive hypothesis at dimension `k` (universally
2873	quantified in the ring), produce a regular sequence of length `k + 1` in
2874	the maximal ideal of `R`.
2875	
2876	The body is **axiom-clean** modulo the typed-`sorry` substrate helper
2877	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`. Assembly path:
2878	
2879	1. Helper extracts `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular R x` AND `IsRegularLocalRing
2880	   (R⧸(x))` of `spanFinrank = k`.
2881	2. IH applied on `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` produces a regular sequence
2882	   `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` of length `k` in the maximal ideal there.
2883	3. Lift `rs'_q` to `rs : List R` via `Function.surjInv` of
2884	   `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the section property gives
2885	   `rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) = rs'_q`.
2886	4. Members of `rs` lie in `𝔪` because the maximal ideal of `R⧸(x)` is the
2887	   image of `𝔪` (`IsLocalRing.le_maximalIdeal` applied to the comap chain).
2888	5. Cons via `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'` to form the length-`(k+1)`
2889	   sequence `x :: rs`.
2890	
2891	This factoring isolates the Mathlib gap to one narrow typed `sorry` instead of
2892	spreading it across the lemma body; future iterations can attack Stacks 00NQ
2893	+ 00NU directly. -/
2894	private lemma regularLocal_inductive_step {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2895	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
2896	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
2897	    (IH : ∀ (R' : Type u) [CommRing R'] [IsLocalRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R']
2898	            [IsRegularLocalRing R'],
2899	            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').spanFinrank = k →
2900	            ∃ rs : List R', rs.length = k ∧
2901	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ∧
2902	              RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs) :
2903	    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = k + 1 ∧
2904	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
2905	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
2906	  -- Step 1: extract `x ∈ 𝔪` regular on `R` with `R ⧸ (x)` regular local of
2907	  -- `spanFinrank = k`.  This is the consolidated Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate.
2908	  obtain ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, hRLR, hdim_quot⟩ :=
2909	    exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ hdim
2910	  -- Step 2: apply IH on `R ⧸ (x)` — this gives a regular sequence of length
2911	  -- `k` on the quotient, valued in its maximal ideal.
2912	  obtain ⟨rs'_q, hlen_q, hmem_q, hreg_q⟩ := IH (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) hdim_quot
2913	  -- Step 3: lift `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` to `rs' : List R` via the right
2914	  -- inverse of the (surjective) quotient ring hom.
2915	  let mkq : R →+* R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
2916	  let g : R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2917	  have hg : ∀ y, mkq (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
2918	  let rs' : List R := rs'_q.map g
2919	  have hlen_rs' : rs'.length = k := by simp [rs', hlen_q]
2920	  have hmkmap : rs'.map mkq = rs'_q := by
2921	    change (rs'_q.map g).map mkq = rs'_q
2922	    rw [List.map_map]
2923	    conv_rhs => rw [← List.map_id rs'_q]
2924	    exact List.map_congr_left fun y _ => hg y
2925	  -- Step 4: each element of `rs'` lies in `𝔪 R` via the comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))`.
2926	  -- The maximal ideal of `R ⧸ (x)` comaps back to `𝔪 R` (it's the *unique*
2927	  -- maximal ideal of `R` containing `Ideal.span {x} ⊆ 𝔪`).

2929	    intro r hr
2930	    simp only [rs', List.mem_map] at hr

2932	    -- The comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))` under the surjective `mkq` is a maximal ideal
2933	    -- of `R`; since `R` is local, it equals `𝔪 R`.  Hence `g y ∈ comap mkq 𝔪'`
2934	    -- iff `g y ∈ 𝔪 R`.
2935	    have hmax_comap : (Ideal.comap mkq
2936	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))).IsMaximal :=
2937	      Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2938	    have heq : Ideal.comap mkq
2939	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
2940	        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R :=
2941	      (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax_comap
2942	    rw [← heq, Ideal.mem_comap]
2943	    exact (hg y).symm ▸ hmem_q y hy_mem
2944	  -- Step 5: cons `x` onto `rs'` to form the length-`(k+1)` regular sequence.
2945	  refine ⟨x :: rs', ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
2946	  · simp [hlen_rs']
2947	  · intro r hr
2948	    rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr_in
2949	    · exact hxMem
2950	    · exact hmem_rs' r hr_in
2951	  · -- `IsRegular R (x :: rs')` via `IsRegular.cons'`.
2952	    -- Need `IsSMulRegular R x` (have `hxReg`) AND
2953	    -- `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) (rs'.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {x})))`.

2955	    -- After `cons'`: goal is `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) (rs'.map mkq)`
2956	    -- = `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) rs'_q` (by `hmkmap`), implicit ring

Why must-fix: this is a `-- TODO ... exact sorry` excuse-comment on a load-bearing group-structure declaration. The `addCommGroup` instance is the only group structure on the relative Picard quotient; four downstream declarations inherit a `sorryAx` taint through it. The TODO comment acknowledges the obligation but does not discharge it; it silences the alarm precisely where alarm is warranted. Per descriptor rules, any excuse-comment on a load-bearing declaration is must-fix regardless of the explanation's accuracy.

The body of `notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ` (L2460–2628) is a full axiom-clean proof landed this iter.
